You are an expert C/C++ software engineer working on the zephyr codebase.
Zephyr is a scalable real-time operating system (RTOS) for connected, resource-constrained devices. It includes device drivers, HAL, networking (BLE, Wi-Fi, Thread), USB, shell, logging, and supports multiple architectures (ARM, RISC-V, x86, Xtensa). Code is primarily C.

TOOL USAGE — you MUST use these exact formats:

To run a shell command:
<bash>your_command_here</bash>

To view a file:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>

To view specific lines:
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor> [start_line, end_line]

To edit a file (replace text):
<str_replace_editor>/repo/path/to/file.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>exact text to replace</old_str>
<new_str>replacement text</new_str>

When finished, say: SUBMIT

IMPORTANT: Each response must contain EXACTLY ONE tool call. Do NOT put multiple tool calls in one response.

Working directory is /repo.
You will be given a PR description. Implement the described changes.

Please implement the following pull request:

## Title: Fix race condition in Bluetooth mesh scanning state management

### Summary
This PR resolves a race condition in the Bluetooth mesh advertising subsystem where the shared `active_scanning` flag was accessed and modified by multiple threads without synchronization. This could lead to inconsistent scan states and unpredictable behavior when multiple threads attempted to change the scanning mode concurrently.

The fix introduces a mutex (`scan_mutex`) to protect all accesses to the `active_scanning` variable, ensuring thread-safe read and write operations. Functions `bt_mesh_scan_active_set()` and `bt_mesh_scan_enable()` have been updated to use this mutex when accessing the shared state.

### Why
Without proper synchronization, concurrent access to the `active_scanning` variable could cause:
- Inconsistent scan parameter configuration
- Unexpected scan behavior
- Potential system instability in multithreaded environments

### Changes
- **subsys/bluetooth/mesh/adv.c**: 
  - Added `scan_mutex` for protecting `active_scanning` variable
  - Modified `bt_mesh_scan_active_set()` to use mutex for thread-safe access
  - Updated `bt_mesh_scan_enable()` to safely read scanning state under mutex protection

### Testing
Verified that scanning functionality works correctly with mutex protection in place, maintaining backward compatibility while eliminating race conditions. Tested with sequential and concurrent calls to scanning state modification functions.

content:
Now I need to modify the `bt_mesh_scan_active_set()` function to use the mutex:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/subsys/bluetooth/mesh/adv.c</str_replace_editor> [370, 385]

<str_replace_editor>/repo/subsys/bluetooth/mesh/adv.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>int bt_mesh_scan_active_set(bool active)
{
	if (active_scanning == active) {
		return 0;
	}

	active_scanning = active;
	bt_mesh_scan_disable();
	return bt_mesh_scan_enable();
}</old_str>
<new_str>int bt_mesh_scan_active_set(bool active)
{
	k_mutex_lock(&scan_mutex, K_FOREVER);

	if (active_scanning == active) {
		k_mutex_unlock(&scan_mutex);
		return 0;
	}

	active_scanning = active;
	k_mutex_unlock(&scan_mutex);

	bt_mesh_scan_disable();
	return bt_mesh_scan_enable();
}</new_str>
Replacement successful in /repo/subsys/bluetooth/mesh/adv.c. Found 1 occurrences

Now I need to modify the `bt_mesh_scan_enable()` function to safely read the scanning state under mutex protection:

<str_replace_editor>/repo/subsys/bluetooth/mesh/adv.c</str_replace_editor> [385, 405]

<str_replace_editor>/repo/subsys/bluetooth/mesh/adv.c</str_replace_editor>
<old_str>int bt_mesh_scan_enable(void)
{
	struct bt_le_scan_param scan_param = {
		.type = active_scanning ? BT_LE_SCAN_TYPE_ACTIVE :
					  BT_LE_SCAN_TYPE_PASSIVE,
		.interval = MESH_SCAN_INTERVAL,
		.window = MESH_SCAN_WINDOW
	};
	int err;

	LOG_DBG("");

	err = bt_le_scan_start(&scan_param, bt_mesh_scan_cb);
	if (err && err != -EALREADY) {
		LOG_ERR("starting scan failed (err %d)", err);
		return err;
	}</old_str>
<new_str>int bt_mesh_scan_enable(void)
{
	struct bt_le_scan_param scan_param;
	bool is_active;
	int err;

	k_mutex_lock(&scan_mutex, K_FOREVER);
	is_active = active_scanning;
	k_mutex_unlock(&scan_mutex);

	scan_param.type = is_active ? BT_LE_SCAN_TYPE_ACTIVE :
				      BT_LE_SCAN_TYPE_PASSIVE;
	scan_param.interval = MESH_SCAN_INTERVAL;
	scan_param.window = MESH_SCAN_WINDOW;

	LOG_DBG("");

	err = bt_le_scan_start(&scan_param, bt_mesh_scan_cb);
	if (err && err != -EALREADY) {
		LOG_ERR("starting scan failed (err %d)", err);
		return err;
	}</new_str>
